TopRequirements.Requirement: Given a user has forgotten their password, when they click Forgot password? on the login page, then they are taken to the password reset form.

Given an existing user enters a valid email and password and clicks Sign in, then they are authenticated and redirected to their account or the last page they were on.

Candidate.ActionDesc: WdRemoteClickAction 'Checkbox Show only eco-friendly products' into 'Checkbox Drill'
ActionDescs: WdRemoteClickAction 'Checkbox Show only eco-friendly products' into 'Checkbox Drill'
SourceTitles: Practice Software Testing - Toolshop - v5.0
TargetTitles: Practice Software Testing - Toolshop - v5.0

TopRequirements.Requirement: Given a user wants to see eco-friendly products, when they check "Only show eco-friendly products" under Sustainability, then the catalogue hides all products that are not labelled as environmentally friendly.
Given a user is browsing products, when they select one or more check boxes under "By category" on the filter panel, then only products belonging to the selected sub-categories are shown.
Given a category contains no products (Special Tools), when the user views that category page, then the page clearly states "No products found" instead of showing an empty grid.
Given a user on the catalogue, when they tick a brand under By brand, then the list displays only items made by the chosen brand.
Given the user is on the home or category page, when they choose a sort option from the Sort drop-down, then the product list is reordered accordingly.
Given the user opens the Categories drop-down in the header, when they select a high-level category, then the application navigates to a dedicated category page listing relevant products.
Given the user navigates to a populated category, then the page displays product cards with images, names, price and CO2 rating.
Given the user clicks a product card, then a detailed page opens showing a large product image, name, brand, price, CO2 rating and descriptive text.
Given the user clicks More information under a related product, then the application navigates to the details page for that product.
Given the user types a search term in the Search field and presses the "Search" button, then the catalogue displays products whose names or descriptions match the query.

Given the catalogue has more products than fit on one page, when the user clicks the page number buttons at the bottom (1 2 3...) or the left/right arrows, then the next or previous page of products is loaded.
Given the user is viewing a product, when they click Add to favourites, then that product is saved to their favourites or wish-list (available once signed in).

Given a user on the product details page, when they click Add to cart, then the selected quantity is added to the shopping cart and the cart icon in the header increments to show the total number of items.
Given the user selects Rentals from the categories menu, then the Rentals page displays cards for available items such as Excavator, Bulldozer and Crane with images and descriptive text.
Given a product is unavailable, then the product card shows an "Out of stock" message and disables actions to add it to the cart.
Given the user is on any page, then a language selector in the header displays the current locale and a drop-down of other options.

Given the language drop-down is open, when the user selects a different language, then the interface text (navigation labels, buttons, form placeholders) switches to that language and persists across pages.
Given the user is on the cart step, when they change the quantity or remove an item, then the cart totals update accordingly.
